market: AVGO: Revenue was $19,311 million versus $14,916 million a year ago (29.5%), and net income was $7,349 million versus $5,503 million (33.5%). Gross profit rose to $13,157 million from $10,145 million, and operating income increased to $8,563 million from $6,260 million. The balance sheet showed $14,174 million of cash and about $66,057 million of total debt at quarter-end.
tech: The filing also confirms fresh financing activity: the company issued $4,500 million of senior unsecured notes during the quarter. Management was aggressive with capital returns, buying back 23 million shares for $7,850 million. The dividend per share rose to $0.65 from $0.59 a year earlier.
risk: Affected names / caveat: AVGO directly. Debt remains large in absolute dollars, so rates/refinancing and integration discipline still matter. The filing discusses risk around AI-related customer purchasing models and financing structures, which could affect revenue timing or cash generation if customer behavior shifts. Quarter-end cash declined from the prior quarter, so watch whether future quarters rebuild liquidity after buybacks and financing moves.
